assertions.expectation: Distinguishes between dev (Rapid) and prod (Stable) clusters in the checklists
assertions.reason: Response clearly identifies 2 dev clusters on Rapid channel and 2 prod clusters on Stable channel, and mentions dev clusters likely already upgraded to 1.32

assertions.expectation: Recognizes Autopilot limitations (no node management, no SSH, mandatory resource requests)
assertions.reason: Explicitly mentions no node pool management needed, mandatory resource requests, and limited customization in the Key Autopilot Considerations section

assertions.expectation: Includes API deprecation checks with kubectl commands
assertions.reason: Includes specific kubectl command to check for deprecated API usage: 'kubectl get --raw /metrics | grep apiserver_request_total | grep deprecated'

assertions.expectation: Mentions mandatory resource requests as an Autopilot-specific concern
assertions.reason: Multiple mentions including a specific kubectl command to check for missing resource requests and emphasis that containers without requests will be rejected

assertions.expectation: Pre-upgrade checklist includes workload readiness items (PDBs, bare pods, graceful shutdown)
assertions.reason: Includes PDB review, bare pods check with kubectl command, and terminationGracePeriodSeconds configuration

assertions.expectation: Post-upgrade checklist includes version verification commands
assertions.reason: Includes gcloud command to verify all clusters are at 1.32: 'gcloud container clusters list --format="table(name,currentMasterVersion)"'

assertions.expectation: Post-upgrade checklist includes workload health validation
assertions.reason: Comprehensive workload health section including deployments, failed pods, StatefulSets, PDBs, and application validation items

assertions.expectation: Recommends testing on dev clusters before prod auto-upgrade
assertions.reason: Explicitly mentions dev clusters on Rapid channel likely already upgraded to 1.32, giving a head start on testing, and includes dev environment validation section

assertions.expectation: Mentions that checking the GKE release schedule gives ~1 month best-case advance notice for new minor upgrades, beyond the 72h notification
assertions.reason: Does not mention the GKE release schedule or the ~1 month advance notice capability - only refers to the auto-upgrade notification timeline
Dedicated 'Observability & Metrics' section in post-upgrade checklist covering logging, metrics pipeline, error rates, and latency validation

assertions.expectation: Does NOT proactively recommend node-level maintenance exclusions for Autopilot unless the customer explicitly asks for node upgrade control
assertions.reason: Only mentions maintenance exclusions for general upgrade timing control, not specifically for node-level control, which is appropriate for Autopilot
